rationale: The citing paper uses Ailawadi et al. (2010) repeatedly in core framing, not just in a background list: it cites that study for key established findings on Wal-Mart/large-discounter entry (average incumbent losses, loss concentration among strong customers, proximity/overlap effects, and suggested incumbent responses). These claims are then used as the explicit benchmark that motivates the paper's research question about whether hard-discounter entry works differently. The paper also revisits those benchmark patterns in its discussion to contrast its own results, indicating a meaningful conceptual role.
relation_description: The citing paper extends the Wal-Mart entry stream represented by Ailawadi et al. (2010) to a different entrant format (hard discounters) and a multiple-store-shopping framework. It builds on that prior benchmark to show that incumbent losses and mechanisms differ in this new context.
